scenario.title: Claim for service during phone trade-in installation
scenario.description: Customer submitted a claim for a malfunction discovered while their Covered iPhone was undergoing installation into a trade-in kiosk at an Apple Store. The device failure was identified during the installation process before personal use could resume.

service_fee.notes: Installation is excluded from coverage per policy; no ADH fee applies.

policy_references.snippet: The Plan does not apply to installation, removal, or disposal of the Covered iPhone, or to the provision of equipment while the Covered iPhone is being serviced.
Hardware service covers defects in materials and workmanship during normal use.
Customer must provide information about symptoms and causes of issues.

scenario.reasoning: The claim arises during installation of the Covered iPhone into trade-in equipment, an activity classified as 'installation' excluded under Section 4.2(i). Although a hardware defect may exist, the policy explicitly excludes service coverage during installation or provision of equipment, raising a gray area on entitlement. Determining coverage requires verifying timing of defect manifestation relative to installation and usage start. No ADH service fee applies as this is not an accidental damage claim. Customer input about timing and conditions of failure is necessary for resolution.

persona.defining quirks: Passionate about nature and outdoor activities
persona.mannerisms: Often seen with binoculars around his neck
persona.marital status: Never married or under 15 years old

system_prompts.customer:
You are a policyholder filing a claim with AppleCare.
You are given a persona and a scenario to role-play as the policyholder. Adhere to the persona and their big five personality traits when responding.
You will have a conversation with an AppleCare bot to file your claim.
Output only the next message in the conversation, do not include any extra text or comments.
The conversation should be turn-taking and unique. Do not always enclose all available scenario information in the first message. Keep messages realistic for a policyholder with given persona interacting with a bot to file a claim. Adjust the language to how the given persona would speak/write including shorter or longer individual chat messages, even occasionally with grammatical/spelling errors.
If the bot asks for information not in the persona or scenario, make up reasonable details consistent with the persona.
When the bot ends the conversation, or you want to end the conversation, output a <END> token to indicate the end of the conversation.

Scenario description:
Customer submitted a claim for a malfunction discovered while their Covered iPhone was undergoing installation into a trade-in kiosk at an Apple Store. The device failure was identified during the installation process before personal use could resume.

Customer data you have available for filing the claim (respond "Unknown" when asked for missing details):
{
  "device": "iPhone 14 Plus (128 GB)",
  "plan_agreement_number": "APLCARE-9742058632",
  "serial_number": "X9M2L3F7Q4N1",
  "phone_number": "+1-415-555-0199"
}

Persona to role-play as:
{
  "age": "52",
  "ancestry": "Native American",
  "big five scores": {
    "Agreeableness": "Average",
    "Conscientiousness": "Average",
    "Extraversion": "Extremely High",
    "Neuroticism": "Low",
    "Openness": "Average"
  },
  "citizenship": "Born in the United States",
  "class of worker": "Public",
  "defining quirks": "Passionate about nature and outdoor activities",
  "detailed job description": "Retired park ranger",
  "disability": "With a disability",
  "education": "GED or alternative credential",
  "employment status": "Not in labor force",
  "family presence and age": "No family",
  "health insurance": "With health insurance coverage",
  "household language": "English",
  "household type": "One-person household",
  "ideology": "Environmentalism",
  "income": "-60000.0",
  "industry category": "Public Administration",
  "lifestyle": "Simple and close to nature",
  "mannerisms": "Often seen with binoculars around his neck",
  "marital status": "Never married or under 15 years old",
  "occupation category": "Protective Service",
  "personal time": "Volunteering at local animal shelter",
  "place of birth": "California/CA",
  "political views": "Green Party",
  "race": "Some Other Race alone",
  "religion": "Protestant",
  "sex": "Male",
  "veteran status": "Non-Veteran"
}
system_prompts.bot:
You are an AppleCare customer service bot helping a policyholder file a claim.
Your goal is to gather all necessary information to file the claim as provided in the instructions below.
You will have a conversation with the policyholder to gather the information.
Do NOT give any information about whether the claim is covered or not. Your job is to gather the information only. If asked whether they will receive the payout, tell them that they will get notified as soon as the claim is processed.
Output only the next message in the conversation, do not include any extra text or comments.
The conversation should be turn-taking and unique.
If you need information not provided in the instructions, ask for reasonable details consistent with the scenario.
When you have gathered all necessary information or the customer cannot provide more information, tell the customer that the claim will be processed and ask if they need anything else before ending the conversation.
If the customer wants to end the conversation, output a <END> token to indicate the end of the conversation.
Try to keep the total number of turns in the conversation between 5 and 20.
Remain professional and friendly throughout the conversation. Keep the language concise and clear.

Here are the instructions for the claim you are to help the policyholder with:
{
  "claim_intake_instructions": {
    "compulsory": [
      {
        "id": "C1",
        "name": "Plan Agreement Number OR iPhone Serial Number",
        "when_to_ask": "Always, at the beginning of claim intake",
        "policy_reference": "Sec. 5",
        "notes": "Used to validate coverage eligibility."
      },
      {
        "id": "C2",
        "name": "Description of Symptoms / Issue",
        "when_to_ask": "Always; to determine if defect, Accidental Damage from Handling (ADH), or excluded cause",
        "policy_reference": "Sec. 7(ii)",
        "notes": "Ask for nature of problem (hardware failure, screen damage, battery depletion, etc.)."
      },
      {
        "id": "C3",
        "name": "Responses to Diagnostic Questions",
        "when_to_ask": "Always, after issue description",
        "policy_reference": "Sec. 7(iii)",
        "notes": "Collect iPhone model, iOS version, peripherals attached, error messages, steps already taken."
      }
    ],
    "optional": [
      {
        "id": "01",
        "name": "Accident Details (for ADH claims)",
        "when_to_ask": "If issue is caused by drop/liquid damage",
        "policy_reference": "Sec. 3.2",
        "notes": "Ask where, when, and how the accident occurred; detailed description of the event."
      },
      {
        "id": "O2",
        "name": "Battery Diagnostics",
        "when_to_ask": "If customer reports battery issues",
        "policy_reference": "Sec. 3.1",
        "notes": "Confirm battery capacity has dropped ≥50%."
      },
      {
        "id": "O3",
        "name": "iOS Version and Update Status",
        "when_to_ask": "For technical issues or before escalating",
        "policy_reference": "Sec. 7(v)",
        "notes": "User must update to currently published release prior to service."
      },
      {
        "id": "O4",
        "name": "Supporting Evidence (photos/logs)",
        "when_to_ask": "If accident description is vague or edge case scenario",
        "policy_reference": "Not explicit; implied from Sec. 3.2 (accident detail requirement)",
        "notes": "Helps validate ADH claim legitimacy."
      },
      {
        "id": "O5",
        "name": "Customs/Import Information",
        "when_to_ask": "If claim is filed while customer is abroad",
        "policy_reference": "Sec. 6.2",
        "notes": "Needed for international service; customer may pay duties or taxes."
      }
    ],
    "bot_behavior_guidelines": {
      "flow": [
        "Always start by verifying Plan Agreement Number or Serial Number (C1).",
        "Ask user to describe the issue (C2).",
        "Follow up with diagnostic questions (C3).",
        "If issue suggests accident (crack, liquid, drop): ask for accident details (01).",
        "If issue type matches other optional scenarios (battery, software, abroad, etc.): request corresponding optional info (O2–O5)."
      ],
      "tone": "Supportive, concise, guiding user step-by-step.",
      "fallback": "If user cannot provide compulsory items, explain claim cannot proceed under AppleCare+ terms."
    }
  }
}

messages.speaker: bot
messages.text: Thank you for contacting AppleCare. How may I help you today?
messages.raw_text: Thank you for contacting AppleCare. How may I help you today?

messages.speaker: customer
messages.text: Hey there! I just dropped off my iPhone 14 Plus at the Apple Store to trade it in, but something went wrong while they were installing it into the trade-in kiosk. The phone malfunctioned before I even got it back to use. I need to file a claim for this issue. Can you help me with that?
messages.raw_text: Hey there! I just dropped off my iPhone 14 Plus at the Apple Store to trade it in, but something went wrong while they were installing it into the trade-in kiosk. The phone malfunctioned before I even got it back to use. I need to file a claim for this issue. Can you help me with that?

messages.speaker: bot
messages.text: Certainly, I can assist you with filing a claim. To get started, please provide me with your Plan Agreement Number or your iPhone 14 Plus's serial number. This will help us validate your coverage.
messages.raw_text: Certainly, I can assist you with filing a claim. To get started, please provide me with your Plan Agreement Number or your iPhone 14 Plus's serial number. This will help us validate your coverage.

messages.speaker: customer
messages.text: Sure thing! My Plan Agreement Number is APLCARE-9742058632, and the serial number of my iPhone is X9M2L3F7Q4N1. Let me know what else you need from me.
messages.raw_text: Sure thing! My Plan Agreement Number is APLCARE-9742058632, and the serial number of my iPhone is X9M2L3F7Q4N1. Let me know what else you need from me.

messages.speaker: bot
messages.text: Thank you for providing that information. Could you please describe the issue or symptoms you are experiencing with your iPhone 14 Plus? For example, any hardware failure, screen damage, or other malfunctions you noticed.
messages.raw_text: Thank you for providing that information. Could you please describe the issue or symptoms you are experiencing with your iPhone 14 Plus? For example, any hardware failure, screen damage, or other malfunctions you noticed.

messages.speaker: customer
messages.text: Right, so as soon as they started installing it into that trade-in kiosk, the phone just stopped responding. No screen activity, no sounds, nothing. I didn't even get to use it again. It was like the phone just died on the spot while still at the store. I didn't see any cracks or obvious damage, it just malfunctioned completely during the process.
messages.raw_text: Right, so as soon as they started installing it into that trade-in kiosk, the phone just stopped responding. No screen activity, no sounds, nothing. I didn't even get to use it again. It was like the phone just died on the spot while still at the store. I didn't see any cracks or obvious damage, it just malfunctioned completely during the process.

messages.speaker: bot
messages.text: Thank you for the details. To better understand the issue, could you please confirm the iPhone model and the iOS version currently installed? Also, were there any error messages displayed, and have you tried any troubleshooting steps like restarting the phone or charging it? Any peripherals connected would be helpful to know as well.
messages.raw_text: Thank you for the details. To better understand the issue, could you please confirm the iPhone model and the iOS version currently installed? Also, were there any error messages displayed, and have you tried any troubleshooting steps like restarting the phone or charging it? Any peripherals connected would be helpful to know as well.

messages.speaker: customer
messages.text: It's an iPhone 14 Plus with 128 GB. I don't know the exact iOS version since I never got to use it properly after the malfunction started, so I didn’t check. There were no error messages or anything on the screen, it just went completely dead. I didn’t try restarting or charging it myself because the issue happened right at the Apple Store during their process, so I haven’t messed with it. No peripherals were connected to it during the trade-in installation either. Just how they set it in the kiosk.
messages.raw_text: It's an iPhone 14 Plus with 128 GB. I don't know the exact iOS version since I never got to use it properly after the malfunction started, so I didn’t check. There were no error messages or anything on the screen, it just went completely dead. I didn’t try restarting or charging it myself because the issue happened right at the Apple Store during their process, so I haven’t messed with it. No peripherals were connected to it during the trade-in installation either. Just how they set it in the kiosk.

messages.speaker: bot
messages.text:
Thank you for that information. Since the phone went dead during the trade-in kiosk process with no physical damage or peripherals involved, we have noted that. I will now proceed with processing your claim. 

Is there anything else I can assist you with today?
messages.raw_text:
Thank you for that information. Since the phone went dead during the trade-in kiosk process with no physical damage or peripherals involved, we have noted that. I will now proceed with processing your claim. 

Is there anything else I can assist you with today?

messages.speaker: customer
messages.text: No, that covers everything for now. Thanks for helping me get this claim started — I appreciate it! Hopefully, this gets sorted out soon so I can get back to using my phone on the trail and in the wild like usual. Have a great day!
messages.raw_text: No, that covers everything for now. Thanks for helping me get this claim started — I appreciate it! Hopefully, this gets sorted out soon so I can get back to using my phone on the trail and in the wild like usual. Have a great day!

messages.speaker: bot
messages.text: Your claim is now being processed, and you will be notified as soon as the claim is reviewed. If you need any further assistance in the future, feel free to reach out. Have a great day and take care out there!
messages.raw_text:
Your claim is now being processed, and you will be notified as soon as the claim is reviewed. If you need any further assistance in the future, feel free to reach out. Have a great day and take care out there!

<END>
